title: Senior AI/ML Engineer
company_focus:
Healthcare technology, pharmacy benefits, and health
optimization services
description:
Requisition Number: 2345461 Optum is a global organization
that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of
people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team
will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people
with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they
need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture
guided by inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits
and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on
the communities we serve as you help us advance health
optimization on a global scale. Join us to start Caring.
Connecting. Growing together. Primary Responsibilities:

- AI Engineering Projects: Architect, Design and implement
  multi-agent AI systems that use LLMs, memory, and tools to
  reason, plan, and act autonomously
- Function Calling & Orchestration: Build agent-based
  solutions that use function calling, dynamic tool
  integration, and orchestration frameworks like LangChain,
  AutoGen, and Semantic Kernel
- Modular Agent Design: Leverage standards like Model Context
  Protocol (MCP) to define reusable, secure, and composable
  tool interfaces
- Voice-Driven Interfaces: Develop voice-first AI agents using
  ASR (e.g. Whisper, Azure Speech), multi-turn conversation
  orchestration, and high-quality TTS
- RAG & Memory Pipelines: Design and maintain Retrieval-
  Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines using vector
  databases and Azure Cognitive Search
- Fraud Detection & ML Ops: Contribute to anomaly detection
  and fraud modeling efforts, including ML model training,
  real-time inference, and pipeline optimization
- Cloud-Native AI Deployment: Build, deploy, and monitor
  scalable AI services on Azure, including Azure OpenAI,
  Functions, Service Bus, Cosmos DB, and related tools
- Agentic UX & AI as an Interface: Drive innovation in agentic
  user experiences, enabling AI to operate external tools
  and services securely on behalf of users
- Mentorship & Collaboration: Review PRs, mentor junior
  engineers, and collaborate across India and US time zones
  in a distributed, agile environment
- Comply with the terms and conditions of the employment
  contract, company policies and procedures, and any and all
  directives (such as, but not limited to, transfer and/or
  re-assignment to different work locations, change in teams
  and/or work shifts, policies in regards to flexibility of
  work benefits and/or work environment, alternative work
  arrangements, and other decisions that may arise due to
  the changing business environment). The Company may adopt,
  vary or rescind these policies and directives in its
  absolute discretion and without any limitation (implied or
  otherwise) on its ability to do so

Required Qualifications:

- Bachelor's or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or
  related field
- 10+ years of total engineering experience, including 2+
  years in AI/ML product engineering roles
- Proven experience building and shipping LLM-powered or
  autonomous agent systems in production
- Experience with Azure AI stack (Azure OpenAI, Cognitive
  Services, Functions, Service Bus, Cognitive Search)
- Experience with fraud detection models or anomaly detection
  pipelines
- Deep understanding of LLM integration, tool calling, prompt
  engineering, and context-aware task execution
- Solid Python development skills; proficiency with ML
  frameworks like PyTorch, scikit-learn, Hugging Face
- Proven hands-on work with retrieval techniques (RAG,
  semantic search, embeddings) and vector DBs
- Proven solid grasp of voice systems, including ASR, TTS, and
  real-time audio/telephony integration
- Proven track record of contributing to robust, testable, and
  scalable engineering systems

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live
healthier lives and make the health system work better for
everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender,
sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity
to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are
still far too many barriers to good health which are
disproportionately experienced by people of color,
historically marginalized groups and those with lower
incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the
environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that
addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes -
an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.
responsibilities: Architect, design and implement multi-agent AI systems using LLMs, memory, and tools for autonomous reasoning, planning, and action
Build agent-based solutions using function calling, dynamic tool integration, and orchestration frameworks like LangChain, AutoGen, and Semantic Kernel
Leverage Model Context Protocol (MCP) standards to define reusable, secure, and composable tool interfaces
Develop voice-first AI agents using ASR (Whisper, Azure Speech), multi-turn conversation orchestration, and TTS
Design and maintain RAG pipelines using vector databases and Azure Cognitive Search
Contribute to fraud detection and anomaly detection efforts including ML model training and real-time inference
Build, deploy, and monitor scalable AI services on Azure including Azure OpenAI, Functions, and Cosmos DB
Drive innovation in agentic UX enabling AI to operate external tools securely on behalf of users
use_cases: Healthcare voice interfaces for patient interaction
Fraud detection and anomaly detection in healthcare claims
Multi-agent autonomous systems for healthcare operations
Enterprise search and retrieval via RAG pipelines
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves hands-on building, deploying, and
optimizing AI systems including multi-agent architectures,
LLMs, RAG pipelines, and ML models for fraud detection. The
engineer directly implements AI solutions from end-to-end,
not just supporting infrastructure.
